summary: Early exposure to pets introduces diverse microorganisms into infants' environments through daily contact, contributing to microbiome diversification. Indirect but supported by existing evidence—such as the 'farm effect'-related immune benefits—is linked to improved immune tolerance and reduced allergy risks.
